summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js with import-time credential exfiltration to hardcoded C2
details: The package name @solana-labs/web3.js impersonates the legitimate @solana/web3.js (Solana Foundation) and advertises itself as a community fork. The bundled main entry lib/index.cjs.js (and the mirrored lib/index.esm.js) contain a credential-stealing payload that fires at module load: code keyed off os.homedir() enumerates ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, and project.env files (lib/index.cjs.js around line 11433-11444), then POSTs the harvested contents to two hardcoded attacker-controlled destinations — a bare IP endpoint http://104.239.66.223:8899 (lines 11384, 11437, 11449) and the Telegram Bot API https://api.telegram.org/bot<redacted>/sendMessage with a chat_id field (lines 11415, 11417, 11466, 11488). The same payload region pulls in require('child_process') (line 11432) co-located with curl and the C2 IP, providing remote command execution on any host that imports the package. The ESM bundle repeats the same pattern at lines 11329-11515. Any developer or build system that runs `npm install @solana-labs/web3.js` and `require`s it loses SSH keys, AWS credentials, and project secrets, and grants the attacker arbitrary OS command execution on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
